steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Rearrange $x^2 + 11x = -8$ to standard form by adding 8 to both sides: $$x^2 + 11x + 8 = 0$$ So $a = 1$, $b = 11$, $c = 8$.

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: Compute the discriminant: $b^2 - 4ac = 121 - 32 = 89$. Substitute into the quadratic formula: $$x = \frac{-11 \pm \sqrt{89}}{2}$$
